rule.name: AppendCharacterWithChar
rule.message: Use a Char literal instead of a single-character String when calling StringBuilder.append().
rule.description:
Passing a single-character string literal to StringBuilder.append(String) forces the JVM to perform a string comparison and then extract the character internally. Using the Char overload — append('x') — is more direct and avoids allocating a temporary String object.
Note: this rule requires kotlin-type-mapper pre-analysis to be available. See the pmd-kotlin documentation for setup instructions.
Kotlin equivalent of Java rule: category/java/performance.xml/AppendCharacterWithChar

property.name: javaEquivalentRule
property.description: Java rule this Kotlin rule is based on; update this rule when the Java rule changes.

property.name: xpath

rule.name: AvoidCallingGcExplicitly
rule.message: Avoid explicit garbage-collection calls; the JVM manages memory automatically.
rule.description:
Calling System.gc() or Runtime.getRuntime().gc() requests a GC cycle but does not guarantee one will run. In practice it introduces unpredictable pauses and can degrade throughput. The JVM's garbage collector is designed to manage memory automatically; manual invocations are almost never correct.
Note: this rule requires kotlin-type-mapper pre-analysis to be available. See the pmd-kotlin documentation for setup instructions.
Kotlin equivalent of Java rule: category/java/performance.xml/AvoidCallingGcExplicitly

property.name: javaEquivalentRule
property.description: Java rule this Kotlin rule is based on; update this rule when the Java rule changes.

rule.name: BigIntegerInstantiation
rule.message: Use BigInteger.ZERO, BigInteger.ONE, or BigInteger.TEN instead of BigInteger.valueOf(0/1/10).
rule.description:
BigInteger.valueOf(0), BigInteger.valueOf(1), and BigInteger.valueOf(10) allocate unnecessarily; the JDK already provides BigInteger.ZERO, BigInteger.ONE, and BigInteger.TEN as cached constants.
Kotlin idiomatic alternatives also include the extension functions: - 0.toBigInteger(), 1.toBigInteger(), 10.toBigInteger() However, the named constants are more readable and allocation-free.
Note: this rule requires kotlin-type-mapper pre-analysis to be available. See the pmd-kotlin documentation for setup instructions.
Kotlin equivalent of Java rule: category/java/performance.xml/BigIntegerInstantiation

property.name: javaEquivalentRule
property.description: Java rule this Kotlin rule is based on; update this rule when the Java rule changes.

property.name: xpath
property.value: //PostfixUnaryExpression[ ( starts-with(pmd-kotlin:nodeText(), 'BigInteger.') or starts-with(pmd-kotlin:nodeText(), 'java.math.BigInteger.') ) and PostfixUnarySuffix/NavigationSuffix[@Identifier='valueOf'] and PostfixUnarySuffix/CallSuffix/ValueArguments/ValueArgument[ pmd-kotlin:nodeText() = '0' or pmd-kotlin:nodeText() = '1' or pmd-kotlin:nodeText() = '10' or pmd-kotlin:nodeText() = '0L' or pmd-kotlin:nodeText() = '1L' or pmd-kotlin:nodeText() = '10L' ] ]

rule.name: UseIndexOfChar
rule.message: Use indexOf(Char) instead of indexOf(String) when searching for a single character.
rule.description:
String.indexOf(String) and String.lastIndexOf(String) perform full substring search when given a single-character argument. The Char overloads — indexOf(Char) and lastIndexOf(Char) — use a simpler linear scan without the overhead of multi-char matching.
Note: this rule requires kotlin-type-mapper pre-analysis to be available. See the pmd-kotlin documentation for setup instructions.
Kotlin equivalent of Java rule: category/java/performance.xml/UseIndexOfChar

property.name: javaEquivalentRule
property.description: Java rule this Kotlin rule is based on; update this rule when the Java rule changes.

property.name: xpath

rule.name: UseStringBuilderLength
rule.message: Use StringBuilder.length instead of StringBuilder.toString().length to avoid creating a temporary String.
rule.description:
Calling toString() on a StringBuilder or StringBuffer just to read its length allocates a temporary String object unnecessarily. StringBuilder and StringBuffer already expose a length property that returns the current character count directly.
Note: this rule requires kotlin-type-mapper pre-analysis to be available. See the pmd-kotlin documentation for setup instructions.
Kotlin equivalent of Java rule: category/java/performance.xml/UseStringBuilderLength

property.name: javaEquivalentRule
property.description: Java rule this Kotlin rule is based on; update this rule when the Java rule changes.

rule.name: AvoidCalendarDateCreation
rule.message: Avoid creating Calendar instances; use the java.time API (LocalDateTime, ZonedDateTime) instead.
rule.description:
java.util.Calendar is a heavyweight, mutable object. Creating a Calendar instance just to obtain the current date or time is expensive and unnecessarily verbose.
Prefer the modern java.time API introduced in Java 8: - java.time.LocalDateTime.now() / ZonedDateTime.now() for date-time values - java.time.Instant.now() for a machine timestamp - java.time.LocalDate.today() for date-only values
Note: this rule requires kotlin-type-mapper pre-analysis to be available. See the pmd-kotlin documentation for setup instructions.
Kotlin equivalent of Java rule: category/java/performance.xml/AvoidCalendarDateCreation

property.name: javaEquivalentRule
property.description: Java rule this Kotlin rule is based on; update this rule when the Java rule changes.

rule.name: AvoidFileStream
rule.description:
FileInputStream/OutputStream/Reader/Writer contain a finalizer that can cause GC pressure. In Kotlin prefer: - File.inputStream() / File.outputStream() for byte streams - File.bufferedReader() / File.bufferedWriter() for character streams - File.readBytes() / File.readText() / File.writeText() for one-shot reads/writes - Files.newInputStream(path) / Files.newOutputStream(path) for the NIO2 approach All of these avoid the finalizer and work naturally with use {}.
Note: this rule requires kotlin-type-mapper pre-analysis to be available. See the pmd-kotlin documentation for setup instructions.
Kotlin equivalent of Java rule: category/java/performance.xml/AvoidFileStream

property.name: javaEquivalentRule
property.description: Java rule this Kotlin rule is based on; update this rule when the Java rule changes.

rule.name: StringInstantiation
rule.message: Avoid creating String objects unnecessarily; use a String literal instead.
rule.description:
Strings are immutable in Kotlin/JVM. Creating String(someString) just copies the reference to a new object; use the string literal or variable directly.
Note: Unlike Java's `new String(str)`, Kotlin's `kotlin.String` does not expose a String(String) copy constructor, so this rule primarily catches Java-interop patterns where the Java constructor is invoked explicitly (e.g. via reflection or a helper that calls `java.lang.String(String)`).
Kotlin equivalent of Java rule: category/java/performance.xml/StringInstantiation

property.name: javaEquivalentRule
property.description: Java rule this Kotlin rule is based on; update this rule when the Java rule changes.

rule.name: StringToString
rule.message: Avoid calling toString() on a String; it returns the same object.
rule.description:
Calling toString() on a String is a no-op; the same String instance is returned. Remove the unnecessary call.
Kotlin equivalent of Java rule: category/java/performance.xml/StringToString

property.name: javaEquivalentRule
property.description: Java rule this Kotlin rule is based on; update this rule when the Java rule changes.
